description: Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced or System Performance (Bank 1). The camshaft position is advancing too much relative to the crankshaft, often caused by a faulty variable valve timing solenoid, timing chain stretch, or oil control valve blockage.
repair_steps: Check engine oil level and condition
Inspect camshaft position sensor for proper operation
Test variable valve timing (VVT) solenoid with a scan tool
Check timing chain tension and condition
Clean or replace oil control valve if necessary
Clear code and verify proper VVT operation at idle and load

description: Crankshaft/Camshaft Position Synchronization Error. The engine control module has detected that the crankshaft and camshaft positions are not synchronized, commonly due to timing chain stretch, broken timing chain, or jumped timing.
repair_steps: Verify crankshaft and camshaft position sensor readings with a scan tool
Inspect timing chain for stretch or damage
Check for broken or jumped timing chain teeth
Verify crankshaft and camshaft sensor alignment
Replace timing chain and components if necessary
Clear code and confirm timing synchronization

description: Heated Oxygen Sensor Control Circuit (Bank 1, Sensor 1). The oxygen sensor heater circuit is not functioning correctly, preventing the sensor from reaching operating temperature quickly.
repair_steps: Inspect oxygen sensor heater wiring and connector for damage or corrosion
Test oxygen sensor heater resistance against specifications
Check heater relay and fuse continuity
Replace oxygen sensor if heater element is faulty
Clear code and verify heater operation with infrared thermometer

description: Heated Oxygen Sensor Heater Circuit Low (Bank 1, Sensor 1). The oxygen sensor heater element is drawing insufficient current, indicating a problem in the heater circuit.
repair_steps: Check oxygen sensor heater wiring for breaks or loose connections
Test heater circuit voltage supply
Measure heater resistance and compare to specifications
Inspect heater relay contacts for arcing or corrosion
Replace oxygen sensor or repair wiring as needed
Clear code and verify heater draws proper current

description: Heated Oxygen Sensor Heater Circuit (Bank 1, Sensor 2). The downstream oxygen sensor heater is not functioning correctly, affecting the secondary exhaust monitoring system.
repair_steps: Inspect downstream oxygen sensor heater connector and wiring
Test heater circuit voltage and ground continuity
Check heater element resistance with a multimeter
Replace oxygen sensor if heater is defective
Clear code and verify proper heater operation

description: Heated Oxygen Sensor Control Circuit (Bank 2, Sensor 1). The oxygen sensor heater on bank 2 (opposite of primary ignition) is not functioning properly.
repair_steps: Locate bank 2 oxygen sensor (opposite side of engine)
Inspect heater circuit wiring and connector integrity
Test heater power supply and ground connections
Measure heater element resistance
Replace oxygen sensor if internal heater is faulty
Clear code and verify heater operation

description: Mass Air Flow (MAF) Sensor Circuit Malfunction. The MAF sensor is detecting a problem in its circuit, preventing accurate air intake measurement for fuel calculation.
repair_steps: Inspect MAF sensor connector for corrosion or loose pins
Check wiring harness for damage between sensor and ECM
Verify power and ground supply to the MAF sensor
Clean MAF sensor carefully with MAF-safe solvent or replace
Clear code and verify normal air flow readings at idle

description: Mass Air Flow Sensor Circuit Low Input. The MAF sensor is reporting a lower air flow than expected for current operating conditions.
repair_steps: Inspect air intake for restriction or blockage
Clean or replace air filter if restricted
Check MAF sensor for dirt, oil contamination, or damage
Verify intake manifold gasket integrity
Test MAF sensor signal voltage with engine running at idle
Replace MAF sensor if faulty

description: Mass Air Flow Sensor Circuit High Input. The MAF sensor is reporting higher air flow than expected, often due to a vacuum leak or sensor malfunction.
repair_steps: Inspect all vacuum hoses for cracks or disconnection
Check intake manifold gaskets for leaks
Verify PCV system operation
Test MAF sensor signal at idle and under load
Clean MAF sensor or replace if reading remains high
Clear code and verify normal air flow measurement

description: Manifold Absolute Pressure/Barometric Pressure Problem. The MAP sensor reading is outside the expected range or not correlating properly with engine load.
repair_steps: Inspect MAP sensor hose for cracks, leaks, or blockage
Check sensor connector for corrosion or loose pins
Test MAP sensor voltage output with engine off and running
Verify vacuum line is properly routed from manifold
Clean or replace MAP sensor if faulty
Clear code and confirm sensor reads barometric pressure at startup

description: Intake Air Temperature (IAT) Sensor Circuit Malfunction. The IAT sensor is not providing valid input to the engine control module.
repair_steps: Inspect IAT sensor connector and wiring for damage
Check sensor power and ground circuits
Test sensor resistance at various temperatures
Verify sensor reading correlates with ambient temperature
Replace IAT sensor if out of specification
Clear code and confirm normal temperature tracking

description: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit Low Input. The IAT sensor is reporting a lower temperature than physically present, indicating sensor or circuit failure.
repair_steps: Inspect IAT sensor wiring for shorts to ground
Check sensor connector contacts for corrosion
Test sensor resistance with multimeter
Verify power supply voltage to sensor
Replace IAT sensor if resistance is out of range
Clear code and verify normal reading at engine start

description: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor Circuit Malfunction. The coolant temperature sensor is not providing valid input for engine temperature control.
repair_steps: Verify coolant level is adequate
Inspect temperature sensor connector and wiring
Check power supply and ground to the sensor
Test sensor resistance at engine warm-up
Replace coolant temperature sensor if faulty
Clear code and confirm proper temperature gauge operation

description: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor Range/Performance Problem. The engine is taking longer than expected to reach operating temperature.
repair_steps: Allow engine to fully warm up and monitor temperature gauge
Compare coolant temperature sensor reading to actual temperature
Check for low coolant level or incorrect coolant type
Inspect thermostat operation and housing
Replace thermostat if stuck open
Clear code and verify warm-up curve returns to normal

description: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor Circuit Low Input. The coolant temperature sensor is reporting lower voltage than expected.
repair_steps: Inspect temperature sensor connector for corrosion
Check wiring for damage or short circuits
Test sensor resistance in hot and cold conditions
Verify proper sensor contact in thermostat housing
Replace coolant temperature sensor if resistance out of spec
Clear code and confirm normal temperature reading

description: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor Circuit High Input. The coolant temperature sensor is reporting higher voltage than expected.
repair_steps: Check for sensor wiring open circuits or breaks
Inspect sensor connector for loose or missing pins
Test sensor resistance value against specifications
Verify ground connection integrity
Replace coolant temperature sensor if faulty
Clear code and confirm proper temperature readings

description: Throttle Pedal Position Sensor/Switch Circuit. The throttle position sensor is not providing consistent input for engine load calculation.
repair_steps: Inspect throttle position sensor connector and wiring
Test sensor voltage throughout pedal range
Check for binding in throttle cable or electronic throttle control
Verify power supply and ground to sensor
Replace throttle position sensor if voltage out of range
Clear code and calibrate electronic throttle control if equipped

description: Oxygen Sensor Circuit No Activity (Bank 1, Sensor 1). The upstream oxygen sensor is not responding to engine operating changes.
repair_steps: Verify oxygen sensor is fully heated and responding
Inspect sensor connector for loose or damaged pins
Check wiring harness for breaks or corrosion
Test sensor voltage output under load
Replace oxygen sensor if not responding to fuel trim changes
Clear code and verify sensor voltage fluctuates at idle

description: Oxygen Sensor Circuit No Activity (Bank 1, Sensor 2). The downstream oxygen sensor is not responding to engine changes or catalyst function.
repair_steps: Ensure downstream oxygen sensor heater has reached operating temperature
Inspect sensor connector and wiring for damage
Check power and ground supply to sensor
Test sensor output voltage for activity
Replace oxygen sensor if heater or sensor element is faulty
Clear code and verify sensor responds to engine transients

description: Oxygen Sensor Circuit Slow Response (Bank 1, Sensor 1). The upstream oxygen sensor is responding too slowly to fuel mixture changes.
repair_steps: Check exhaust for leaks upstream of oxygen sensor
Verify oxygen sensor is fully heated and aged
Inspect sensor for carbon buildup or contamination
Test sensor response time with a lab scope if available
Replace oxygen sensor if response time exceeds specifications
Clear code and confirm normal sensor response at idle

description: Oxygen Sensor Circuit No Activity (Bank 1). The primary oxygen sensor bank is showing insufficient electrical activity.
repair_steps: Check oxygen sensor heater circuit operation
Inspect sensor connector for corrosion or water intrusion
Verify oxygen sensor is properly installed and grounded
Test heater and sensor element with multimeter
Replace oxygen sensor if heater or element is open
Clear code and verify sensor voltage activity at idle

description: Oxygen Sensor Heater Circuit (Bank 1, Sensor 1). The upstream oxygen sensor heater is not functioning correctly.
repair_steps: Check oxygen sensor heater fuse and relay
Inspect heater circuit wiring for breaks or loose connections
Test heater power supply voltage
Measure heater element resistance with multimeter
Replace oxygen sensor if heater element is open or shorted
Clear code and verify heater draws current when powered

description: Oxygen Sensor Heater Circuit (Bank 1, Sensor 2). The downstream oxygen sensor heater is not responding to control commands.
repair_steps: Locate downstream oxygen sensor (after catalytic converter)
Inspect heater connector and wiring for damage
Test heater circuit voltage supply
Check heater element resistance
Replace oxygen sensor if heater is faulty
Clear code and verify heater operates when engine starts

description: Fuel Injector Circuit (Bank 1). One or more fuel injectors on bank 1 are not functioning properly.
repair_steps: Check fuel injector wiring and connectors
Test injector coil resistance against specifications
Verify injector control signal from engine control module
Check fuel pressure to confirm adequate supply
Clean fuel injectors with fuel system cleaner or replace
Clear code and verify fuel injector pulse at idle

description: Fuel Injector Circuit (Bank 2). The fuel injection system on bank 2 (opposite side) is not functioning correctly.
repair_steps: Locate bank 2 fuel injectors (opposite side of primary)
Inspect fuel injector wiring and connectors
Test injector coil resistance and control signals
Verify fuel pressure supply to bank 2 injectors
Clean or replace faulty fuel injectors
Clear code and confirm injection pulse at idle and load

description: Fuel Trim System Too Rich (Bank 1). The engine control module has detected that bank 1 is running richer than target after all correction attempts.
repair_steps: Check for vacuum leaks in intake system
Verify oxygen sensor operation and heater function
Inspect fuel pressure regulator for proper operation
Test fuel injector spray pattern and flow rate
Check air mass flow sensor for contamination
Clear code and verify fuel trims return to acceptable range

description: System Too Rich (Bank 1). The engine is running too rich (excess fuel), commonly caused by a faulty oxygen sensor, fuel pressure regulator, or defective fuel injector.
repair_steps: Verify oxygen sensor heater is functioning
Check fuel pressure and compare to specifications
Inspect fuel pressure regulator for vacuum leak or stuck plunger
Test fuel injector resistance and flow rate
Check mass airflow sensor for proper operation
Clear code and verify rich condition has improved

description: System Too Lean (Bank 2). The engine is running too lean on bank 2, often due to vacuum leaks, weak fuel pump, or mass airflow sensor issue.
repair_steps: Perform smoke test for vacuum leaks
Check fuel pressure and pump operation
Inspect mass airflow sensor for contamination
Test oxygen sensor heater and sensor response
Verify fuel injectors are not restricted
Clear code and confirm lean condition is resolved

description: System Too Rich (Bank 2). Bank 2 of the engine is operating with an excessive fuel to air ratio.
repair_steps: Verify bank 2 oxygen sensor operation
Check fuel pressure at bank 2 fuel rail
Inspect fuel pressure regulator for leaks or malfunction
Test bank 2 fuel injectors for proper flow
Verify air intake is not restricted on bank 2 side
Clear code and monitor fuel trims for bank 2

description: Fuel Temperature Sensor Circuit. The fuel temperature sensor is not providing valid input to the engine control module.
repair_steps: Inspect fuel temperature sensor connector and wiring
Check power supply and ground to the sensor
Test sensor resistance at various fuel temperatures
Verify sensor location is in fuel line or fuel rail
Replace fuel temperature sensor if out of specification
Clear code and confirm normal sensor operation

description: Fuel Rail Pressure Sensor Circuit. The fuel pressure sensor is not functioning correctly, preventing accurate fuel delivery control.
repair_steps: Inspect fuel pressure sensor connector and wiring
Check sensor power supply and ground
Test sensor output voltage at various engine loads
Verify fuel pressure is within normal range
Replace fuel pressure sensor if signal is out of range
Clear code and confirm proper fuel pressure control

description: Fuel Rail Pressure Sensor Range/Performance. The fuel rail pressure reading is not matching expected values for current operating conditions.
repair_steps: Verify fuel pump operation and delivery volume
Check fuel filter for restriction or blockage
Test fuel pressure sensor output voltage under load
Inspect fuel pressure regulator for proper operation
Check fuel rail for pressure leaks
Replace fuel pressure sensor if readings remain inconsistent

description: Fuel Rail Pressure Sensor Low Input. The fuel rail pressure is lower than expected or the sensor is reporting low voltage.
repair_steps: Check fuel pump operation and output
Inspect fuel filter condition and flow rate
Test fuel pressure sensor voltage output
Verify fuel pressure regulator is not stuck closed
Check for fuel leaks in fuel rail or lines
Replace fuel pump or sensor if pressure remains low

description: Fuel Rail Pressure Sensor High Input. The fuel pressure sensor is reporting higher than normal pressure or sensor output voltage is too high.
repair_steps: Check fuel pressure against specification with manual gauge
Inspect fuel pressure regulator for stuck or leaking valve
Test fuel pressure sensor voltage with multimeter
Verify fuel return line is not restricted or blocked
Check for fuel leaks in fuel rail connections
Replace fuel pressure sensor or regulator as needed

description: Injector Circuit Malfunction. The engine control module has detected a problem in the fuel injector circuit.
repair_steps: Check fuel injector wiring harness for damage or corrosion
Test injector coil resistance on all cylinders
Verify injector control signal from engine control module
Check fuel pressure supply to injectors
Test injectors individually for proper spray pattern
Replace faulty fuel injectors and clear code

description: Cylinder 1 Injector Circuit. The fuel injector for cylinder 1 is not functioning correctly.
repair_steps: Inspect cylinder 1 injector connector and wiring
Test injector coil resistance against specifications
Verify injector control signal from ECM
Check fuel pressure supply to cylinder 1
Test injector spray pattern under fuel pressure
Replace cylinder 1 injector if faulty

description: Cylinder 2 Injector Circuit. The fuel injector for cylinder 2 is not responding properly.
repair_steps: Locate cylinder 2 and inspect injector connector
Test injector resistance and control signal
Verify fuel pressure reaches cylinder 2 injector
Check wiring for breaks or short circuits
Test injector spray pattern and flow rate
Replace cylinder 2 injector if defective

description: Cylinder 3 Injector Circuit. The fuel injector serving cylinder 3 is malfunctioning.
repair_steps: Identify cylinder 3 location on the engine
Inspect injector connector for corrosion or damage
Test injector coil resistance and control signal
Verify fuel pressure supply to the injector
Check wiring harness for breaks or loose connections
Replace cylinder 3 injector if resistance out of spec

description: Cylinder 4 Injector Circuit. The fuel injector for cylinder 4 is not operating correctly.
repair_steps: Locate cylinder 4 fuel injector on engine
Inspect injector and connector for damage
Test injector resistance with multimeter
Verify ECM control signal to the injector
Check fuel pressure and return flow
Replace cylinder 4 injector if coil resistance is open or shorted

description: Engine Overtemperature Condition. The engine coolant temperature has exceeded safe operating limits.
repair_steps: Stop the engine immediately and allow to cool
Check coolant level and condition when engine is cool
Inspect radiator for blockage or collapsed cooling fins
Test thermostat and water pump operation
Check cooling fan motor and clutch operation
Verify temperature sensor is not faulty and replace if needed

description: Throttle/Pedal Position Switch (B) Circuit. The secondary throttle position sensor is not providing consistent input.
repair_steps: Locate throttle position sensor B on throttle body
Inspect connector and wiring for damage
Test sensor voltage output through full throttle range
Check power supply and ground connections
Verify sensor is properly aligned with throttle shaft
Replace throttle position sensor if voltage out of range
